extraction.one_line_summary: THOMAS Geraint attacked solo with 700m remaining on technical corner finish, held off COLBRELLI Sonny by centimeters after what appeared a miscalculated move.
tactical_patterns: THOMAS exploited sharp technical corners in finale to attack solo from GC group, offsetting yesterday's poor TT with 10-second bonus.
Breakaway self-destructed early when ČERNÝ and MULLEN attacked with 60km remaining, forcing ASGREEN back to peloton, neutralizing time threat.
PÖSTLBERGER's team controlled all day to protect 1-second GC lead over LUTSENKO, preventing breakaway riders gaining dangerous time gaps.
Late-race corners created acceleration opportunities too difficult for sprint trains to control, favoring punchy GC riders over pure sprinters like KRISTOFF.

rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: attacked from technical corner with perfect timing despite positioning mid-field, showed recovery and tactical sharpness post-TT disappointment
rider_signals.COLBRELLI Sonny: positive: fastest finisher in race so far, nearly closed 700m gap in final sprint, outperformed traditional sprinters on lumpy finale
rider_signals.ASGREEN Kasper: negative: early crash weakened him, peloton controlled breakaway specifically to neutralize his time threat, forced back before finale
rider_signals.KRISTOFF Alexander: negative: positioned poorly mid-field for technical sprint finish, unable to contest despite being designated top sprinter
rider_signals.PÖSTLBERGER Lukas: positive: held yellow by just 1 second over LUTSENKO through team control, four days in yellow exceeds expectations
rider_signals.BERNARD Julien: neutral: aggressive in breakaway but neutralized when ASGREEN pulled back, strong Monument winner unable to survive to finale
rider_signals.CRADDOCK Lawson: neutral: caught late by peloton after being in breakaway, no impact on final result
